ROCm Fallback Verification on GFX900: A Comprehensive Analysis

The Radeon Open Compute (ROC) architecture is a popular parallel computing platform that has gained immense popularity in recent years due to its high performance and efficiency. The ROCm software stack, developed by NVIDIA, provides a comprehensive set of tools for developers to build applications on top of the ROC architecture. However, there are some limitations associated with using ROCm on certain hardware platforms, such as the Radeon RX 500 series (GFX900). In this note, we will examine the ROCm fallback verification on GFX900 and provide a comprehensive analysis of its performance, efficiency, and limitations.
